  "text": "The Envoy Stats system is composed of several interconnected components implementing a hierarchical metrics collection and distribution architecture:\n\n## Core Stats Hierarchy (envoy/stats/)\nThe stats interface layer defines the public API with `Scope` (envoy/stats/scope.h) as the central abstraction for managing counters, gauges, histograms, and text readouts. The `Tag` and `StatNameTag` types (envoy/stats/tag.h) represent metadata extracted from metric names. The `TagExtractor` interface (envoy/stats/tag_extractor.h) enables tag extraction from metric names, while `TagProducer` (envoy/stats/tag_producer.h) orchestrates the extraction process. The `Sink` interface (envoy/stats/sink.h) defines the contract for metric sinks to receive metric snapshots for output.\n\n## Stats Store Implementations (source/common/stats/)\n\n### IsolatedStoreImpl (isolated_store_impl.h/cc)\nProvides a simple, non-thread-safe stats store with `IsolatedScopeImpl` implementing the `Scope` interface. This is used for testing and isolated contexts where stats don't need to be shared across threads.\n\n### ThreadLocalStoreImpl (thread_local_store.h/cc)\nThe primary production stats store with thread-local optimization. The nested `ScopeImpl` struct implements the `Scope` interface and provides thread-safe access to counters, gauges, histograms, and text readouts through a central cache and thread-local caching mechanisms.\n\n### Symbol Table and Stat Name Interning (symbol_table.h/cc)\nImplements the core stat name interning system using a byte-encoded symbol table for memory efficiency. The `StatNamePool` class provides RAII-based management for interned stat names, while `StatNameDynamicPool` enables dynamic stat name creation without symbol table locks. This system reduces memory overhead by sharing common tokens across stat names.\n\n## Tag Extraction System (tag_extractor_impl.h/cc, tag_producer_impl.h/cc, tag_utility.h/cc)\nThe `TagExtractorImplBase` implements regex-based tag extraction from metric names. The `TagProducerImpl` orchestrates multiple extractors and manages performance optimizations like prefix token matching. `TagUtility` provides helper functions for tag-statname joining and manipulation. This system enables extracting structured metadata from flat metric names without requiring explicit tag specification at metric creation time.\n\n## Metric Implementations (histogram_impl.h/cc, metric_impl.h/cc, allocator_impl.h/cc)\nThe `MetricImpl` template and derived classes provide reference-counted storage for metrics. `ParentHistogramImpl` and `ThreadLocalHistogramImpl` implement histogram recording with thread-local collection and periodic merging for performance. The `AllocatorImpl` manages symbol table interning and metric allocation.\n\n## Stat Sink Dispatch Layer (source/extensions/stat_sinks/)\n\n### UDP/TCP Statsd Sinks (common/statsd/statsd.h/cc, statsd/config.h/cc)\nThe `UdpStatsdSink` and `TcpStatsdSink` classes implement the `Sink` interface for dispatching metrics to statsd collectors. The `TagFormat` system (tag_formats.h/cc) enables different tag representation formats (Datadog, Graphite, etc.) with specialized config implementations in dog_statsd/ and graphite_statsd/ directories.\n\n### Metrics Service Sink (metrics_service/)\nThe `GrpcMetricsServiceImpl` provides gRPC-based metric export with proto descriptor generation for streaming metrics to external services.\n\n### OpenTelemetry Sink (open_telemetry/)\nThe `OpenTelemetryImpl` sink exports metrics in OpenTelemetry format with protocol buffer descriptor support.\n\n### Hystrix and Wasm Sinks\nSpecialized sink implementations for Hystrix dashboard integration and WebAssembly-based custom metric processing.\n\n## Prometheus Output (source/server/admin/prometheus_stats.h/cc)\nThe `PrometheusStatsFormatter` converts the internal stat representation (with extracted tags) into Prometheus exposition format, handling metric name sanitization and label formatting to comply with Prometheus naming conventions.\n\n## Complete Architecture Flow\n1. Metrics are created and recorded via `Scope` interfaces\n2. Stat names are interned through `SymbolTable` and `StatNamePool` for memory efficiency\n3. Tags are automatically extracted from metric names via `TagExtractor` implementations\n4. `ThreadLocalStoreImpl` maintains central and thread-local caches for performance\n5. During flush cycles, `MetricSnapshot` captures metric state\n6. Multiple `Sink` implementations receive snapshots and format output:\n   - `UdpStatsdSink`/`TcpStatsdSink` for statsd format with configurable tag formats\n   - `GrpcMetricsServiceImpl` for gRPC metric export\n   - `OpenTelemetryImpl` for OpenTelemetry format\n   - `PrometheusStatsFormatter` for Prometheus scraping\n\nThis modular architecture separates metric collection (Scope hierarchy), name interning (SymbolTable), tag extraction (TagExtractor), storage (Store implementations), and output (Sink implementations), enabling efficient memory usage and flexible metric distribution to multiple backends simultaneously."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Envoy Stats Scope and Tag Architecture

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that implement the stats system: the Stats::Scope hierarchy, the tag extraction from metric names, the stat name interning (StatNamePool), and the stat sink dispatch for Prometheus and statsd output.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the org domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that implement the stats system. Let me start by exploring the workspace structure and then systematically search for the relevant components.
Now let me create a comprehensive list and output the findings.

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Envoy stats system and created the answer file. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Summary

I identified **77 C++ source and header files** implementing the Envoy stats system across three main categories:

### 1. **Core Stats Interface** (envoy/stats/)
Headers defining the public API including `Scope`, `Tag`/`StatNameTag`, `TagExtractor`, `TagProducer`, and `Sink` interfaces.

### 2. **Stats Implementation** (source/common/stats/)
- **IsolatedStoreImpl/IsolatedScopeImpl**: Simple non-thread-safe stats store
- **ThreadLocalStoreImpl/ScopeImpl**: Production stats store with thread-local optimization
- **SymbolTable & StatNamePool**: Byte-encoded stat name interning for memory efficiency
- **TagExtractorImplBase & TagProducerImpl**: Regex-based tag extraction system
- **MetricImpl, ParentHistogramImpl, ThreadLocalHistogramImpl**: Reference-counted metric storage
- **AllocatorImpl**: Symbol table interning and metric allocation

### 3. **Stat Sinks** (source/extensions/stat_sinks/)
- **UdpStatsdSink/TcpStatsdSink**: Statsd format with configurable tag formats (Datadog, Graphite)
- **GrpcMetricsServiceImpl**: gRPC-based metric export
- **OpenTelemetryImpl**: OpenTelemetry format export
- **PrometheusStatsFormatter**: Prometheus exposition format output

The architecture uses a modular design where metrics flow from creation → stat name interning → tag extraction → storage → multi-sink dispatch, enabling efficient memory usage and flexible metric distribution to multiple backends.

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json`.

arguments.description: Find all header and source files in common stats implementation
Find all header and source files in stats headers directory
Find all stat sink implementation files
Find Scope class definitions in stats headers
Find Scope implementations in common stats
Find all Scope class implementations excluding tests
Get complete list of source/common/stats files
Get complete list of stat sink implementation files

  "text": "The Envoy Stats system is composed of several interconnected components implementing a hierarchical metrics collection and distribution architecture:\n\n## Core Stats Hierarchy (envoy/stats/)\nThe stats interface layer defines the public API with `Scope` (envoy/stats/scope.h) as the central abstraction for managing counters, gauges, histograms, and text readouts. The `Tag` and `StatNameTag` types (envoy/stats/tag.h) represent metadata extracted from metric names. The `TagExtractor` interface (envoy/stats/tag_extractor.h) enables tag extraction from metric names, while `TagProducer` (envoy/stats/tag_producer.h) orchestrates the extraction process. The `Sink` interface (envoy/stats/sink.h) defines the contract for metric sinks to receive metric snapshots for output.\n\n## Stats Store Implementations (source/common/stats/)\n\n### IsolatedStoreImpl (isolated_store_impl.h/cc)\nProvides a simple, non-thread-safe stats store with `IsolatedScopeImpl` implementing the `Scope` interface. This is used for testing and isolated contexts where stats don't need to be shared across threads.\n\n### ThreadLocalStoreImpl (thread_local_store.h/cc)\nThe primary production stats store with thread-local optimization. The nested `ScopeImpl` struct implements the `Scope` interface and provides thread-safe access to counters, gauges, histograms, and text readouts through a central cache and thread-local caching mechanisms.\n\n### Symbol Table and Stat Name Interning (symbol_table.h/cc)\nImplements the core stat name interning system using a byte-encoded symbol table for memory efficiency. The `StatNamePool` class provides RAII-based management for interned stat names, while `StatNameDynamicPool` enables dynamic stat name creation without symbol table locks. This system reduces memory overhead by sharing common tokens across stat names.\n\n## Tag Extraction System (tag_extractor_impl.h/cc, tag_producer_impl.h/cc, tag_utility.h/cc)\nThe `TagExtractorImplBase` implements regex-based tag extraction from metric names. The `TagProducerImpl` orchestrates multiple extractors and manages performance optimizations like prefix token matching. `TagUtility` provides helper functions for tag-statname joining and manipulation. This system enables extracting structured metadata from flat metric names without requiring explicit tag specification at metric creation time.\n\n## Metric Implementations (histogram_impl.h/cc, metric_impl.h/cc, allocator_impl.h/cc)\nThe `MetricImpl` template and derived classes provide reference-counted storage for metrics. `ParentHistogramImpl` and `ThreadLocalHistogramImpl` implement histogram recording with thread-local collection and periodic merging for performance. The `AllocatorImpl` manages symbol table interning and metric allocation.\n\n## Stat Sink Dispatch Layer (source/extensions/stat_sinks/)\n\n### UDP/TCP Statsd Sinks (common/statsd/statsd.h/cc, statsd/config.h/cc)\nThe `UdpStatsdSink` and `TcpStatsdSink` classes implement the `Sink` interface for dispatching metrics to statsd collectors. The `TagFormat` system (tag_formats.h/cc) enables different tag representation formats (Datadog, Graphite, etc.) with specialized config implementations in dog_statsd/ and graphite_statsd/ directories.\n\n### Metrics Service Sink (metrics_service/)\nThe `GrpcMetricsServiceImpl` provides gRPC-based metric export with proto descriptor generation for streaming metrics to external services.\n\n### OpenTelemetry Sink (open_telemetry/)\nThe `OpenTelemetryImpl` sink exports metrics in OpenTelemetry format with protocol buffer descriptor support.\n\n### Hystrix and Wasm Sinks\nSpecialized sink implementations for Hystrix dashboard integration and WebAssembly-based custom metric processing.\n\n## Prometheus Output (source/server/admin/prometheus_stats.h/cc)\nThe `PrometheusStatsFormatter` converts the internal stat representation (with extracted tags) into Prometheus exposition format, handling metric name sanitization and label formatting to comply with Prometheus naming conventions.\n\n## Complete Architecture Flow\n1. Metrics are created and recorded via `Scope` interfaces\n2. Stat names are interned through `SymbolTable` and `StatNamePool` for memory efficiency\n3. Tags are automatically extracted from metric names via `TagExtractor` implementations\n4. `ThreadLocalStoreImpl` maintains central and thread-local caches for performance\n5. During flush cycles, `MetricSnapshot` captures metric state\n6. Multiple `Sink` implementations receive snapshots and format output:\n   - `UdpStatsdSink`/`TcpStatsdSink` for statsd format with configurable tag formats\n   - `GrpcMetricsServiceImpl` for gRPC metric export\n   - `OpenTelemetryImpl` for OpenTelemetry format\n   - `PrometheusStatsFormatter` for Prometheus scraping\n\nThis modular architecture separates metric collection (Scope hierarchy), name interning (SymbolTable), tag extraction (TagExtractor), storage (Store implementations), and output (Sink implementations), enabling efficient memory usage and flexible metric distribution to multiple backends simultaneously."
}
